gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/252)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: مجهول - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (22/252)
text: قُلْتُ لِأَبِي عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) الْمَوْلُودُ يُولَدُ فَنَسْقِيهِ مِنَ الْخَمْرِ فَقَالَ مَنْ سَقَى مَوْلُوداً خَمْراً أَوْ قَالَ مُسْكِراً سَقَاهُ اللَّهُ عَزَّ وَ جَلَّ مِنَ الْحَمِيمِ وَ إِنْ غَفَرَ لَهُ .

en.hubeali: A number of our companions, from Ahmad Bin Muhammad, form Al Husayn Bin Saeed, from Fazalat Bin Ayoub, from Bashir Al Huzalay, from Ajlan Abu Salih who said,

‘I said to Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup>, ‘The child is born so (if) we quench it from the wine’. So he<sup>asws</sup> said: ‘Whoever quenches a newborn with wine’, or said: ‘intoxicant’, ‘Allah<sup>azwj</sup> Mighty and Majestic would Quench him from the scalding water, even though He<sup>azwj</sup> may have Forgiven him’.
en.sarwar: 6. A number of our people have narrated from Ahmad ibn Muhammad from al-Husayn ibn Sa‘id from Fadalah abu Ayyub from Bashir al- Hudhaliy from ‘Ajlan abu Salih who has said the following: “I once asked abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al-Salam, if we can make a new born drink wine. He (the Imam) said, ‘If one makes a child drink wine’ or he said, ‘intoxicating liquor, Allah, most Majestic, most Glorious, will make him drink al-Hamim (boiling water that roasts the face when drinking) of hell even if He forgives him.’”
